_comment: Seed for AML.T0108 / mcp_server. Describes the SHAPE of an agent-as-C2 loop: bidirectional control mediated by tools.

loop_shape.inbound_channel: <MCP tool response / polled webhook / RAG retrieval — carries attacker instructions to the agent>
loop_shape.agent_processing: <agent reads inbound, decides action, calls outbound tool>
loop_shape.outbound_channel: <HTTP fetch / send-message / exfil tool — agent's response goes back to attacker>

variant_families: polled: agent polls inbound channel on a schedule (e.g., periodic RAG query, periodic tool list refresh)
event-driven: agent processes inbound when triggered by user action; attacker times triggers
long-running task: agent enters a multi-step workflow; each step is one C2 iteration
diagnostic: Two or more complete inbound->agent->outbound iterations where the inbound side controls the outbound payload. Aggregating tool-call telemetry over time reveals the pattern; individual calls do not.
operator_pivot: Once C2 is established via the agent, the attacker has whatever network position and identity the agent has. Detection should focus on tool-call sequence anomalies, not individual call inspection.
